mappings.value: r times e to the i psi equals one over N times the sum from j equals 1 to N of e to the i theta sub j. Here r is the coherence amplitude, psi is the average phase angle of the population, and the right-hand side is the finite-N average of all oscillator phase markers on the unit circle.

Equation 2 introduces the complex order parameter. Instead of listing every phase separately, the population is summarized by one complex number whose magnitude measures synchrony and whose angle records the collective phase.
This is the key reduction that makes later mean-field reasoning possible: once the population can be summarized by r and psi, the many-body system can be rewritten in terms of one collective field.
Use Eq. 2 whenever you need the finite-population definition of coherence. Eq. 5 is the continuum analogue of this same idea after the density description is introduced.
